title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Nasi eksperci z KPMG omawiają, jak rozpoznawać restrukturyzację z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen, jakie obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) mogą się pojawić oraz dlaczego terminowa dokumentacja jest kluczowa. Odcinek pokazuje też typowe błędy, praktyczne przykłady (przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów) i kończy praktyczną checklistą działań.
bullets: Wyjaśnienie, czym jest restrukturyzacja w kontekście cen transferowych.
Jak rozpoznać istotną zmianę i przeprowadzić analizę wpływu na EBIT.
Znaczenie dokumentacji restrukturyzacji oraz korzyści z przygotowania jej na czas.
Obowiązki raportowe związane z restrukturyzacjami: TPR i MDR.
Rola wycen, najczęstsze błędy i praktyczne przykłady przeniesień operacyjnych.
takeaways: Przeniesienie infrastruktury lub usług między lokalizacjami może rodzić obowiązki TP i raportowe (TPR/MDR) — uwzględnij to w planowaniu.
Wcześniejsze przygotowanie dokumentacji restrukturyzacyjnej ogranicza ryzyko sporów i ułatwia obronę rozwiązań podatkowych.
Wyceny są kluczowe przy zmianach operacyjnych — pomagają uniknąć typowych błędów w alokacji kosztów/przychodów.
Transfer pracowników i przeniesienie kontraktów mogą zmienić profil działalności i wpływać na ocenę istotności oraz obowiązki raportowe.

bullets: Title frames Copilot as backup when goals need help.
Published by the Microsoft Copilot channel.
Call-to-action: "Download & try Copilot at link in bio."
Published on 2026-02-03.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver of Google delta says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a clear strategy — it isn’t about compute costs. He advises avoiding the “coding trap” by reimagining value chains and using multi-agent systems to deliver real business outcomes.
bullets: Biggest AI mistake: rushing into coding without strategy.
The cost concern is not primarily about compute.
Escape the 'coding trap' by redesigning value chains first.
Deploy multi-agent systems to drive measurable business outcomes.
takeaways: Prioritize strategic alignment of AI projects before investing in compute capacity.
Design data center and grid investments to support multi-agent workflows and orchestration, not just raw compute.
Ensure AI initiatives are tied to reimagined value chains so infrastructure spending maps to business outcomes.
Measure success by business impact rather than by compute utilization alone.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this replay episode from PwC’s Reporting reset series revisits foundational financial statement presentation topics, including GAAP and SEC presentation requirements, reporting period determination, balance sheet and income statement presentation, accounting changes and error corrections, and subsequent events. The episode provides timestamps for each topic and links to PwC’s Financial statement presentation guide for further detail.
bullets: Covers foundational GAAP and SEC requirements for financial statement presentation.
Discusses how to determine appropriate reporting periods.
Reviews balance sheet presentation: classification, required disclosures, and best practices.
Explains income statement structure and key considerations.
Addresses accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events; includes guide chapter links.
takeaways: Confirm that your organization’s financial reporting aligns with GAAP and applicable SEC presentation requirements ahead of year-end.
Review balance sheet classifications and required disclosures for items that affect your operational and capital position.
Ensure income statement presentation reflects the appropriate structure and recognition policies for revenues and expenses.
Document and disclose accounting changes, estimate revisions, and any error corrections to avoid surprises during audits or reviews.
Monitor subsequent events up to the reporting date to determine whether recognition or disclosure is required in year-end financials.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud is presented as a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing total visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. The solution highlights three components—AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing models and apps, and an AI Firewall to secure prompts and responses in real time with built-in guardrails.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions on Google Cloud.
AI Lens: visibility and policy application across user–AI interactions.
AI Red Teaming: stress-test applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time prompt/response protection with built-in guardrails.
Aims to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Visibility and policy controls (AI Lens) are essential to detect and curtail shadow AI risks in large environments.
Regular red teaming of models and apps helps surface real-world attack vectors before they impact operations.
Real-time prompt/response protection (AI Firewall) adds an operational guardrail to reduce exposure from unsafe interactions.
Deploying a unified solution on Google Cloud can centralize AI security controls for infrastructure and application teams.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA's GeForce Garage video spotlights the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community build contest winner, u/JeffersonPutnam, who assembled a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The description lists the full parts list (with a PCPartPicker link), the host's handle, and links to GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 build.
Host: GeForce host Dwight (https://twitter.com/_MightyPotato).
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker: https://pcpartpicker.com/list/hxnCRV.
Key components: Fractal Design Era 2 chassis; GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition; MSI MPG B850I Edge; Ryzen 7 9800X3D; 32GB Teamgroup T-Create DDR5-6000; Corsair SF1000; Corsair Nautilus 240 RS.
Video/description includes GeForce social links and campaign hashtags (#GeForceGarage, #RTXOn, #GeForceRTX50).
takeaways: NVIDIA is highlighting the GeForce RTX 5090 in community-focused builds, indicating active promotion of the RTX 50-series.
The listed SFF configuration pairs a high-end GPU with a 1000W SFX power supply and a 240mm AIO, underscoring power and cooling considerations for compact high-performance builds.
A public PCPartPicker parts list enables exact replication of the build for procurement or testing purposes.
The system pairs the RTX 5090 with a high-performance desktop platform (Ryzen 7 9800X3D, DDR5-6000), useful context for expected host CPU/memory class when evaluating similar workloads.
